Title: Inactivation of RPX1 in Arabidopsis confers resistance to Plutella xylostella through the accumulation of the homoterpene DMNT

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Repels and kills Plutella xylostella larvae by damaging the larval peritrophic matrix/midgut; contributes to pest resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inactivation of RPX1 increases DMNT accumulation and confers resistance to P. xylostella; RPX1 acts as a negative regulator of DMNT-mediated defence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rpx1-1 (SALK_131503) knockdown mutant shows reduced larval preference, increased larval mortality, reduced pupation, and less leaf damage. 2. RPX1 complementation restores susceptibility and lowers DMNT to wild-type-like levels. 3. RPX1 inactivation elevates PEN1 expression and DMNT accumulation. 4. RPX1 protein is rapidly degraded after P. xylostella infestation or wounding, suggesting a rapid inducible defence mechanism. 5. rpx1-1 shows no growth penalty and maintains higher seed production under infestation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; complementation (RPX1pro:RPX1/rpx1-1); qRT-PCR; GC-MS metabolite profiling; insect preference/no-choice bioassays; immunoblotting of RPX1-GFP/FLAG
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in different tissues; transcript not significantly induced by 6–24 h larval infestation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant growth penalty reported; under infestation, rpx1-1 has much higher seed production than wild type
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves/aerial parts after root-to-shoot transport; biosynthesis linked mainly to roots
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: RPX1 is a promising genome-editing target to improve pest resistance via elevated DMNT without typical growth trade-offs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RPX1 protein, but not mRNA, is rapidly degraded by infestation and wounding; exact mechanism by which RPX1 controls PEN1 is unclear and may involve mRNA stability or translation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Sesquiterpenoid and triterpenoid biosynthesis; DMNT biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to arabidiol, a precursor leading to bioactive DMNT via CYP705A1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Repels and kills P. xylostella larvae by damaging the peritrophic matrix/midgut; promotes pest resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher PEN1 expression promotes DMNT accumulation and resistance to P. xylostella; PEN1 knockdown in rpx1-1 suppresses DMNT accumulation and resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PEN1 is upregulated in rpx1-1 and induced by P. xylostella infestation. 2. PEN1 knockdown in rpx1-1 strongly reduces DMNT accumulation under basal and infested conditions. 3. PEN1 overexpression in Col-0 markedly increases DMNT and enhances larval mortality while reducing pupation. 4. PEN1 knockdown alleviates larval midgut/PM damage seen with rpx1-1, confirming causality.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; artificial miRNA knockdown in rpx1-1; 35S overexpression; GC-MS for DMNT; insect mortality/pupation bioassays; Smurf assay; histology of peritrophic matrix
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key DMNT biosynthetic enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in roots; very low in leaves; induced by P. xylostella infestation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in roots; very low in leaves; induced by infestation, especially higher in rpx1-1 after 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PEN1 overexpression is reported to increase seed yield under infested and uninfested conditions; no obvious growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: DMNT detected in leaves/aerial parts; PEN1 expression mainly in roots
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PEN1 is a useful engineering target to elevate DMNT and enhance pest resistance, potentially without fitness cost
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites CYP705A1 as the downstream enzyme producing bioactive DMNT from arabidiol, but CYP705A1 itself was not experimentally tested here.
